# Context Engineering for LLMs
<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

> Dimension 4 of 8 | See [../citations.md](../citations.md) for full source details

## Key Finding

Context preparation is the primary determinant of AI-assisted development productivity. The swing between unstructured AI use (-19% [3]) and spec-driven approaches (+20-55%) represents a 40-75 percentage point gap entirely determined by context engineering quality.

---

## Defining Context Engineering

Context engineering emerged in mid-2025 as the successor to prompt engineering [55]:

- **Tobias Lutke (Shopify CEO):** "The art of providing all the context for the task to be plausibly solvable by the LLM" [55]
- **Andrej Karpathy:** "Apps build contexts (meticulously) for LLMs to solve their custom tasks" [55]
- **Anthropic:** "Building with language models is becoming less about finding the right words...and more about answering the broader question of 'what configuration of context is most likely to generate our model's desired behavior?'" [51]

The key distinction: prompt engineering is about individual instructions; context engineering is about the complete information environment.

---

## Quantitative Impact of Context Quality

| Evidence | Without Context | With Context | Delta | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| First-attempt accuracy | 70-82% | 95%+ | +13-25 pp | [57] |
| Code generation accuracy | Baseline | +17.35% | +17.35 pp | [16] |
| Developer productivity | -19% (slower) | +20-55% faster | 40-75 pp | [3] vs [1][37] |
| Context miss rate | N/A | 65% say AI misses context | N/A | [30] |
| Hallucination + confidence | 88% low confidence | 3.8% high confidence | N/A | [30] |

---

## Context Architecture Tools

| Tool/Format | Scope | Maintainer | Adoption | Key Feature |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AGENTS.md | Tool-agnostic | Linux Foundation | 60K+ projects [56] | Tool-agnostic standard |
| CLAUDE.md | Claude Code | Anthropic | 67% of AI-configured repos [39] | Hierarchical, auto-loaded |
| .cursor/rules/ | Cursor IDE | Cursor | Widely used | Glob-based auto-attach |
| spec-kit | SDD workflow | GitHub | Experimental | Constitution -> Specify -> Plan -> Tasks |
| Repomix | Repo-to-prompt | Open source | Active | Single-file packaging |
| MCP | Tool/data protocol | Anthropic / LF | Growing | Protocol standard |

### Instruction Limits

"Frontier thinking LLMs can follow ~150-200 instructions with reasonable consistency" (widely cited in CLAUDE.md best practices guides; see Anthropic docs and Nick Babich/UX Planet). The AGENTS.md standard recommends 150 lines or fewer [56].

---

## Spec-Driven Development (SDD) ROI

The spec-kit [53] workflow: **Constitution -> Specify -> Plan -> Tasks**

| Metric | With SDD | Without SDD | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| First-attempt accuracy | 95%+ | 70-82% | [57] |
| AI-authored code share | Up to 90% | Variable | SDD guides |
| Google migration time reduction | 50% | Baseline | Google case |
| Airbnb test migration | 6 weeks | 1.5 years (est.) | Airbnb case |
| METR experienced devs | N/A (unstructured) | -19% slower | [3] |

**Key insight:** The METR study [3] tested unstructured AI use. The spec-driven approach is specifically designed to address the causes of that slowdown -- inadequate context, unclear requirements, debugging loops.

---

## Context Window Constraints

| Constraint | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Performance at long contexts | Degrades to 60-70% of peak performance scores | [58] |
| Optimal utilization target | 40-60% | [54] |
| Performance degradation onset | 80-90% window usage | Multiple [58] |
| Primacy/recency effect | Middle-of-context info poorly utilized | [58] |
| "Lost in the middle" | Beginning and end weighted more heavily | Multiple |

**Estimation implication:** Tasks must be sized to fit within 40-60% of the context window. Larger tasks require multi-pass approaches with compaction overhead.

---

## Context Debt

"AI contextual debt is the accumulated cost of AI making decisions or generating assets without having the full, current, business-aware context" [77].

Key dynamics:
- AI generates at 10x velocity, context dissipates at 10x rate [77]
- The "repetition tax": re-entering context per interaction vs. maintaining persistent context [77]
- 61% report AI code "looks correct but is unreliable" [28]
- One team stopped all feature development for 3 weeks due to incomprehensible AI-generated codebase [43]

**Prevention:** Invest in context architecture (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, specs) as infrastructure, not overhead.

---

## Estimation Framework for Context Effort

Five phases to budget explicitly:

| Phase | Scope | Time Estimate | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|
| Context architecture setup | CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, conventions | Hours to days | Per project |
| Per-task context preparation | Research, spec writing, code identification | 15-60 min | Per task |
| Implementation with compaction | AI coding + context management overhead | +20-40% overhead | Per task |
| Review and quality gates | Human review, testing, correction cycles | 30-50% of implementation | Per output |
| Context maintenance | Updating context files as project evolves | Minutes per change | Ongoing |

## Gaps and Limitations

- No controlled study directly measures context preparation time vs. output quality across multiple projects
- The 95% first-attempt accuracy claim [57] appears aspirational rather than from a controlled study
- Context window utilization research is sparse and model-specific
- No tool currently estimates context preparation effort as part of task estimation
- The 150-200 instruction limit [CLAUDE.md research] is not validated in peer-reviewed research
